topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an irritating persistence, mirroring the low thrum of annoyance in Leo’s chest. He stared at the printed accusation, the ink seeming to mock his diligent hours spent wrestling with dense historical texts. Every line detailing the alleged intellectual theft felt like a physical weight pressing down on his shoulders. He pinched the bridge of his nose, a sharp, involuntary twitch escaping his control. "This is absurd," he muttered under his breath, the sound barely audible above the shuffling of nearby students. He shoved the paper into his backpack with unnecessary force, the zipper protesting loudly. He needed coffee, black and scalding, and silence—a luxury currently denied to him by this whole ordeal.

[story 2]

"Look, I just don't get it," Sarah sighed, running a hand through her already messy hair. She paced the small corner of the common room, her jaw tight. The accusation hung in the air, sharp and unwelcome. Liam just leaned back in his chair, his arms crossed tightly over his chest, his gaze fixed on a distant, dusty bookshelf as if the answer to the world's problems lay hidden behind the spines of old volumes. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped him, a sound laced with profound dissatisfaction. He picked at a loose thread on his sweater, his movements stiff and resentful, clearly wishing the entire situation would simply dissolve into thin air.

[story 3]

My jaw felt clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. The accusation landed like a cold stone in my stomach, instantly stealing any remaining patience. I didn't look up from the desk, focusing instead on the microscopic imperfections in the wood grain of my laptop. Every word in the official notice seemed deliberately obtuse, designed only to irritate. I tapped a rhythm on the keyboard, a sharp, uneven beat that betrayed the churning frustration inside me. "Fine," I finally managed, the word clipped and devoid of warmth, "let's just get this over with." I pushed the chair back, the scrape of metal on linoleum sounding like a declaration of war against the entire academic system.
